heroHeadline: Somalia on the Brink: April 14 Parliamentary Mandate Deadline, NISA Kills 9 Al-Shabaab, WFP Food Aid Crisis
heroSubtitle: Somalia's constitutional crisis is racing toward its critical flashpoint — parliamentary mandates expire April 14, forcing a showdown between the FGS and the opposition 'Council for the Future of Somalia' (Puntland, Jubbaland, opposition). NISA killed 9 Al-Shabaab fighters and destroyed an IED factory in Lower Shabelle (Apr 4); elite Gorgor Commandos captured senior Al-Shabaab battalion commander at Mubarak (Apr 3). AFRICOM reached nearly 50 airstrikes in Q1 2026 — a record pace. Meanwhile, WFP food assistance is at a critical shortfall: 6.5 million Somalis face hunger, the 76% funding gap threatens aid to the most vulnerable.
footerNote: Data sourced from ACLED, OCHA, UNSOM, AFRICOM, UN Monitoring Group, and major international outlets. Casualty figures contested where noted.
